name: Bobby Tucker
abstract: Bobby Tucker (born Robert Nathaniel Tucker; January 8, 1923 – April 12, 2007) was a pianist and arranger during the jazz era from the 1940s into the 1960s. He is most famous for being Billie Holiday's accompanist from 1946 to 1949 and Billy Eckstine's from 1950 to 1993.

concerts.label: Billy Eckstine, George Shearing Quintet, and The All Stars
Billy Eckstine, George Shearing Quintet, and The All Stars

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Gibbs
about_quotes.evidence: Billy Eckstine, the headliner with Bobby Tucker played piano, and Sarah Vaughan and Roy Haynes and Jimmy Jones on piano... For two months, can you do better than that if you want to play with people?
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Gibbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 5, 2021 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: John Levy
about_quotes.evidence: Bobby called me to ask me if I would join the group to accompany Billie at this particular concert... I said, hey, I’d be happy to do that, and especially to work with him.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Levy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: John Levy
about_quotes.evidence: In 1948 I got a call from Bobby Tucker to join him to work with Billie Holiday.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Levy interviewed by Monk Rowe, Majesty of the Seas, May 31, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Mays
about_quotes.evidence: I learned watching and listening to Jimmy Rowles accompany singers and Mike Wofford and Bobby Tucker, and the list goes on.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Mays interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 5, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Horace Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: I followed a guy that's an excellent pianist. He was with her for quite some time -- Bobby Tucker... so I took his place

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: And Bobby Tucker would go in the booth, you know, and be like the producer. And we'd both begin to learn Billy so well
